methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Tytan Technologies, a Series A defense and security company founded by Balazs Nagy and Batuhan Yumurtaci, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 8 private-stage peers in the Defense & Security sector, supplemented by 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The peer set includes later-stage companies such as Dream Security ($3.0B Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B Series B), Code Metal ($1.2B Series B), Castelion ($1.0B Series B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B Series B), alongside additional private peers including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach for this stage and sector. The 8-company peer cohort yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M at the low end to $3.0B at the high end. This method directly anchors the valuation to observed market pricing for comparable-stage defense technology firms.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $850M to $1.1B, with a median point of $1.0B. This reflects Tytan's positioning within a cohort of Series A/B-stage defense firms. Key uncertainties include limited granularity on Stage A-specific pricing within the peer set—most sampled comparables are Series B—and the company's specific technology differentiation relative to peers. Additional revenue, growth trajectory, and customer concentration data would strengthen conviction around positioning within the derived range.
